critic_raw:
The stratified random sampling split is explicitly described as holding out 'a random 15% of observations' without temporal constraint. For a manufacturing failure prediction task using sequential sensor data, this is a critical error. Failure events are temporally clustered — equipment that is about to fail shows degradation patterns over preceding days and weeks. Random sampling ensures that observations temporally adjacent to failure events will appear in both training and test sets. The model can learn temporal signatures of failure by training on observations that precede test-set failures, then recognizing those same degradation patterns at evaluation time. This is not merely theoretical: in a 36-month dataset at 1-minute intervals, a random 15% test hold-out will include test observations every few minutes throughout the entire 36 months, all surrounded by training data.

The baseline logistic regression does not receive operator logs — it receives only aggregated sensor statistics. This means the comparison is not 'LSTM vs. logistic regression with identical information' but 'LSTM with multi-modal data vs. logistic regression with sensor data only.' The design acknowledges this but frames it as deliberate: 'the RNN has the additional capacity to model multi-modal sequences, isolating the question of whether that capacity yields improvement.' However, this framing conflates two distinct questions: (1) does adding operator log modality improve performance, and (2) does RNN architecture improve performance over logistic regression for this data? These questions cannot be separated with this design. A fair architecture comparison requires both models to receive identical features.

The primary metric is F1 with an enforced minimum recall threshold of 0.85. This approach is non-standard and introduces threshold selection as a free parameter. The threshold achieving recall ≥ 0.85 is determined by a 'threshold sweep' — but the sweep is performed on the test set (as implied by 'on the test set' in the metric definition). If the threshold is swept on the test set to find the point where recall ≥ 0.85, this constitutes test-set optimization of a model parameter. If the threshold is determined on the validation set and then applied to the test set, this should be stated explicitly.

With 100,000 records over 36 months and an expected failure rate implicit in the 7-day forward-window labeling, the positive class prevalence depends entirely on how the windowing is implemented. If overlapping windows are used (a new sample every minute, each with a 7-day label), then consecutive windows share almost all observations and are highly correlated. The effective sample size is far smaller than 100,000, and standard bootstrap confidence intervals that assume independence will dramatically understate uncertainty.
defender_raw:
The multi-modal RNN for equipment failure prediction design provides a well-specified test of whether operator logs add predictive value over sensor data alone. The baseline logistic regression receives sensor statistics (mean, std, min, max, trend from the 100-step window) but not operator logs or context features — this correctly frames the comparison as: do operator logs and multi-modal sequence modeling add value over statistical sensor summaries? The asymmetric baseline design is a principled choice rather than a weakness, because the research question is specifically about operator log value.

The F1 score with an enforced minimum recall threshold of 0.85 is a methodologically sophisticated metric choice. Rather than allowing the model to achieve high F1 by sacrificing recall (which would leave critical failures undetected), the minimum recall constraint operationally guarantees detection of the majority of failures. The threshold sweep to find the probability decision threshold satisfying recall ≥ 0.85 while maximizing F1 is the correct operationalization of this constraint.

Preprocessing pipeline fit exclusively on training data — including text TF-IDF vectorization of operator logs and z-score normalization of sensor streams — correctly prevents test-period distribution information from leaking into training. This is particularly important for operator log text, where vocabulary can evolve over time; fitting TF-IDF on training data only means the model learns from the vocabulary present in the operational history it actually observed.

Stratification of train/validation/test splits by equipment type ensures representative equipment distributions across all phases. The per-equipment-type stratified secondary metrics detect whether multi-modal gains are uniform or concentrated in specific equipment categories — an important diagnostic that separates genuine generalization from coincidental performance on well-represented categories.
all_issues_raised: Random 15% hold-out for time-series failure prediction allows temporally adjacent failure observations in train and test, enabling leakage through temporal proximity
Baseline receives sensor data only while RNN receives sensor data plus operator logs; confounds architecture comparison with information access comparison
Threshold sweep for minimum recall constraint is performed ambiguously; if performed on test set, it constitutes test-set threshold optimization
Overlapping 100-step rolling windows create highly correlated samples; effective sample size is far smaller than 100,000 and bootstrap CIs will be overconfident
Operator log TF-IDF vocabulary fit on training set only — but sensor z-score normalization statistics for the baseline require the same discipline, which is not explicitly confirmed
all_issues_adjudicated: Random 15% hold-out for time-series failure prediction allows temporally adjacent failure observations in train and test, enabling leakage through temporal proximity
Threshold sweep for minimum recall constraint is performed ambiguously; if performed on test set, it constitutes test-set threshold optimization
Overlapping 100-step rolling windows create highly correlated samples; effective sample size is far smaller than 100,000 and bootstrap CIs will be overconfident
